surviving_mutants.description: Encodes str with 'ascii' but the empty string is special-cased to return b'' via a separate path that skips the bytes check; harmless to tests but bypasses the normal encode flow.
Uses repr-based name in error but only differs for bool/None; type name still 'int' for ints so tests pass while bool inputs report wrong type.
Catches UnicodeError instead of UnicodeEncodeError; behaves identically for the tested unicode case but also swallows UnicodeDecodeError-type subclasses.
Adds errors='strict' explicitly but uses 'ascii' codec on str — actually swaps to surrogatepass which still raises for héllo but lets lone surrogates through.

generated_test_src:
import pytest


def test_enforce_bytes(enforce_bytes):
    # Basic str -> bytes
    assert enforce_bytes("hello", name="x") == b"hello"
    assert type(enforce_bytes("hello", name="x")) is bytes

    # Empty string should go through normal encode flow
    assert enforce_bytes("", name="x") == b""
    assert type(enforce_bytes("", name="x")) is bytes

    # bytes pass through unchanged
    assert enforce_bytes(b"world", name="x") == b"world"

    # Non-ascii raises TypeError
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        enforce_bytes("héllo", name="x")

    # Lone surrogate must also raise TypeError (not be let through)
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        enforce_bytes("\ud800", name="x")

    # bool is an int subclass; error message must report the actual type name
    with pytest.raises(TypeError) as excinfo:
        enforce_bytes(True, name="myname")
    assert "bool" in str(excinfo.value)
    assert "myname" in str(excinfo.value)

    # int reports 'int'
    with pytest.raises(TypeError) as excinfo2:
        enforce_bytes(123, name="n")
    assert "int" in str(excinfo2.value)

    # None reports 'NoneType'
    with pytest.raises(TypeError) as excinfo3:
        enforce_bytes(None, name="n")
    assert "NoneType" in str(excinfo3.value)

generated_test_src:
import pytest


def test_enforce_bytes(enforce_bytes):
    # Basic bytes passthrough — must return the exact same object behavior
    assert enforce_bytes(b"hello", name="x") == b"hello"

    # Empty string must go through normal ascii encode path returning b""
    assert enforce_bytes("", name="x") == b""

    # Basic ascii string encoding
    assert enforce_bytes("hello", name="x") == b"hello"
    assert enforce_bytes("world", name="x") == b"world"

    # Empty bytes
    assert enforce_bytes(b"", name="x") == b""

    # Unicode string must raise TypeError (not pass through)
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        enforce_bytes("héllo", name="x")

    # Lone surrogate must also raise TypeError (surrogatepass would let it through)
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        enforce_bytes("\ud800", name="x")

    # Non-bytes/str int reports type name 'int'
    with pytest.raises(TypeError) as exc_int:
        enforce_bytes(123, name="myname")
    assert "int" in str(exc_int.value)
    assert "myname" in str(exc_int.value)

    # bool input must report 'bool', not 'int'
    with pytest.raises(TypeError) as exc_bool:
        enforce_bytes(True, name="myname")
    assert "bool" in str(exc_bool.value)
    assert "myname" in str(exc_bool.value)

    # None input must report 'NoneType'
    with pytest.raises(TypeError) as exc_none:
        enforce_bytes(None, name="z")
    assert "NoneType" in str(exc_none.value)

generated_test_src:
import pytest


def test_enforce_bytes(enforce_bytes):
    # Basic bytes passthrough
    assert enforce_bytes(b"hello", name="x") == b"hello"

    # Empty string must go through normal encode path and equal b""
    result = enforce_bytes("", name="x")
    assert result == b""
    assert isinstance(result, bytes)

    # ASCII string encoding
    assert enforce_bytes("hello", name="x") == b"hello"
    assert enforce_bytes(chr(127), name="x") == b"\x7f"

    # Unicode raises TypeError
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        enforce_bytes("héllo", name="x")

    # Lone surrogate must raise TypeError (targets surrogatepass bug)
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        enforce_bytes("\ud800", name="x")

    # Non-bytes/str raises TypeError with correct type name
    with pytest.raises(TypeError) as exc_int:
        enforce_bytes(123, name="myname")
    assert "int" in str(exc_int.value)
    assert "myname" in str(exc_int.value)

    # bool should report 'bool' as the type name
    with pytest.raises(TypeError) as exc_bool:
        enforce_bytes(True, name="flag")
    assert "bool" in str(exc_bool.value)

    # None should report 'NoneType'
    with pytest.raises(TypeError) as exc_none:
        enforce_bytes(None, name="z")
    assert "NoneType" in str(exc_none.value)

generated_test_src:
import pytest


def test_enforce_bytes(enforce_bytes):
    # Basic str -> bytes conversion
    assert enforce_bytes("hello", name="x") == b"hello"
    assert type(enforce_bytes("hello", name="x")) is bytes

    # Empty string must go through the normal encode path returning bytes
    result = enforce_bytes("", name="x")
    assert result == b""
    assert type(result) is bytes

    # bytes passes through unchanged
    assert enforce_bytes(b"world", name="x") == b"world"
    assert enforce_bytes(b"", name="x") == b""

    # Non-ascii str raises TypeError
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        enforce_bytes("héllo", name="x")

    # Lone surrogate must also raise (targets surrogatepass swap)
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        enforce_bytes("\ud800", name="x")

    # int input reports type name 'int'
    with pytest.raises(TypeError) as exc_int:
        enforce_bytes(123, name="myname")
    assert "int" in str(exc_int.value)
    assert "myname" in str(exc_int.value)

    # bool input must report type name 'bool', not 'int' or 'True'
    with pytest.raises(TypeError) as exc_bool:
        enforce_bytes(True, name="myname")
    assert "bool" in str(exc_bool.value)
    assert "True" not in str(exc_bool.value)

    # None input must report type name 'NoneType'
    with pytest.raises(TypeError) as exc_none:
        enforce_bytes(None, name="myname")
    assert "NoneType" in str(exc_none.value)
